feedback.text: As a EU citizen and consumer, lover of video games, both as an art but also as a way to help various people, including me, with sometimes their loneliness, sadness, but also when we are exhausted or stressed. Video games can become a healthy part of a person's life and having companies taking away what is a way to people communicate with each other while doing something even if they are countries apart, i believe its awful, especially because it doesn't need to be like that, there are ways to let people enjoy what they paid for. To take away what gives people stories that brighten their day, kindles new friendships or just gives a bit of nostalgia of a game they played when they were kids. Games have so much potential not just as a way to tell stories to teach everyone, but can be therapeutic or just downright useful as a teaching tool. The EU has a unique chance to set global standards for consumer protection in the gaming sector. Save our games, save our multiple lives!
